Home About Services Preventive Dentistry Restorative Dentistry Emergency Dentistry Sedation Dentistry Special Needs Dentistry Contact Us FAQ SCHEDULE NOW SCHEDULE NOW We provide full scope Pediatric Dentistry We take pride in providing compassionate, comprehensive dental care for children of all ages.

We’re dedicated to bringing the latest technology and techniques to deliver top-notch care that’s tailored to your child’s unique needs. Where we stand out is our focus and specialization in the following: Minimally Invasive Dentistry Minimally Invasive Dentistry is a modern treatment philosophy that focuses on preventing and treating tooth decay without removing tooth structure. This option is made possible with early detection.

Tooth Anomalies Tooth anomalies include: Congenitally Missing Teeth, Supernumerary (Extra) Teeth, Enamel Hypoplasia or Hypomineralization, Peg Laterals, and Tooth Fusion or Gemination. Early detection and management of these anomalies can minimize their impact on your child’s oral health and improve esthetics. + MORE Call us at 214. 833. 8964 for more information. info@andsmiles.

How it works: If it’s determined by our team of experts that your newborn requires a tongue or lip tie release, also known as a Frenectomy, we use a soft tissue laser to precisely cut the tissue and free the movement of the tongue and lip. This state-of-the-art technique allows optimal healing and minimal bleeding for your child, while avoiding the need for General Anesthesia. We’ll continue to be a resource for you following the procedure to ensure your child’s post-operative success.

Emergency Services Accidents and emergencies happen, we are here to help. If your child experiences a toothache, facial swelling, chipped or knocked out tooth, our doctors are equipped to take care of your child. They’ve had extensive training at Children’s Medical Center Dallas Emergency Room in managing all dental emergencies, big or small.

Options for all: As Board-Certified Pediatric Dentists, we specialize in behavior management techniques such as Nitrous Oxide and Oral Conscious Sedation. Additionally, we partner with highly trained Anesthesiologists to provide a safe and convenient option for In-office IV Sedation. For those with Special HealthCare Needs (SHCN) requiring hospitalization for dental treatment, we both remain credentialed and maintain hospital privileges at Children’s Medical Center Dallas.

Tongue & Lip Evaluation We focus on a conservative and collaborative approach when assessing and treating tongue and lip ties. We work alongside a team of specialists (Lactation Consultants, Myofunctional Therapists, Pediatricians) to ensure all other interventions have been made prior to surgical intervention.

Behavior Management Our goal is to build a foundation of confidence and trust for a lifetime of dental care for your child. There are times that the extent of treatment your child needs exceeds what a child that age can tolerate. That’s when customized care is most important. We’ll consider all factors to ensure that together we select the best option for your child.

We offer Silver Diamine Fluoride (SDF), Hall Crowns, and Icon Resin Infiltrations (for pesky white spot lesions after braces). White Crowns & White Fillings We understand esthetic options are important for patients of all ages. We’re experienced in all esthetic material options including: Zirconia, Pre-veneered Porcelain and Resin-Based. We are happy to discuss which option is best for your child.

A pediatric frenectomy is a simple dental procedure used to release a tight or restrictive frenulum, commonly referred to as tongue-tie (ankyloglossia) or lip-tie. Tongue-tie affects the tissue under the tongue and can limit movement needed for feeding and speech. Lip-tie affects the tissue connecting the upper lip to the gums and may interfere with feeding, oral hygiene, or tooth spacing.

Frenectomies can be performed on infants, toddlers, and older children depending on symptoms and functional concerns. Is a frenectomy painful for children. The procedure is quick and well tolerated. We use gentle techniques and comfort measures to minimize discomfort. What signs indicate my child may need a frenectomy. Common signs include difficulty breastfeeding, bottle feeding challenges, speech delays, limited tongue movement, or gaps between the front teeth due to lip-tie.
